title: Perform a System Bag Out
summary: Use the overall system bag out control to initiate a system-wide bag out that closes all destinations and exchanges totes or chutes that contain parcels. The source also states that any exchange already in process must finish.
when_to_use: Use when a system-wide bag out is needed and the intended behavior is to close all destinations and exchange totes or chutes that still contain parcels.
not_for: Do not use if the intended action is to stop an exchange that is already in process; the source states in-process exchanges must finish.
Do not use when a leg-specific bag out is desired; the source notes bag out can also be chosen by leg of the sorter, which is a different option.
Do not use as a cancel-bag-out action; the source distinguishes manual cancel bag out from system bag out.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the overall system bag out control, select the system bag out option, and verify the documented bag out behavior.

safety_notes: The source describes this as a user-selectable control action but does not provide additional safety controls or warnings.
Do not assume an exchange already in process will stop; the source states it must finish.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the bag out control system
Visibility to bag out status or exchange activity

access_required: Access to the overall system bag out control
system_preconditions: The operator can view the overall system control area or bag out control screen.
The sorter/system is in a state where a system bag out can be selected.

visual_references.description: Overall system bag out control slide describing system bag out behavior and related bag out options.
Overall system main overview screen showing bag out among the right-hand side controls.

steps.title: Access the overall system bag out control
steps.purpose: Reach the control area where the system-wide bag out action is available.
steps.instruction: Open or navigate to the overall system control area and locate the bag out control for the system.
steps.expected_result: The overall system bag out control is visible and available for selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can see the overall system control area and identify the bag out control.
steps.failure_condition: The bag out control cannot be located or the operator cannot access the overall system control area.

actions.label: Open overall system controls
actions.exact_action: Access the bag out control for the overall system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overall system main overview screen with bag out listed among the right-hand side controls.
The overall system bag out control slide describing the available bag out behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the overall system bag out control is not visible or accessible.

steps.title: Select system bag out
steps.purpose: Initiate the system-wide bag out action rather than another bag out mode.
steps.instruction: Select the system bag out option for the sorter from the overall system bag out control.
steps.expected_result: The system bag out action is initiated.
steps.healthy_condition: The system bag out option is selected from the overall system bag out control.
steps.failure_condition: A different option is selected, or the system bag out action does not initiate.

actions.label: Choose system bag out
actions.exact_action: Select the system bag out option for the sorter.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overall system bag out control content describing system bag out as a selectable option.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system bag out option is unavailable.
Escalate if the interface behavior does not match the documented system bag out description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange chutes or totes with parcels in them.

steps.title: Confirm all destinations close
steps.purpose: Verify the documented system-wide effect of the bag out action on destinations.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the bag out action closes all destinations.
steps.expected_result: All destinations are closed as part of the system bag out.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed system behavior matches the source statement that system bag out closes all destinations.
steps.failure_condition: Destinations remain open or behavior does not match the documented system bag out behavior.

actions.label: Verify destination closure
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the bag out action closes all destinations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text stating that system bag out will close all destinations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if all destinations do not close.
Escalate if observed behavior does not match the documented system bag out description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange chutes or totes with parcels in them.

steps.title: Verify occupied totes or chutes are exchanged
steps.purpose: Verify the documented exchange behavior for destinations or containers that still contain parcels.
steps.instruction: Verify that totes or chutes with parcels in them are exchanged as part of the bag out process.
steps.expected_result: Occupied totes or chutes containing parcels are exchanged.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed exchange activity matches the source statement that system bag out exchanges chutes or totes with parcels in them.
steps.failure_condition: Occupied totes or chutes are not exchanged, or exchange behavior differs from the source description.

actions.label: Verify exchange activity
actions.exact_action: Verify that totes or chutes with parcels in them are exchanged as part of the bag out process.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text stating that system bag out exchanges chutes or totes with parcels in them.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if occupied totes or chutes are not exchanged.
Escalate if observed bag out behavior does not match the documented system bag out description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange chutes or totes with parcels in them.

steps.title: Observe in-process exchanges continue to completion
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented exception that an exchange already in process is not canceled by this action.
steps.instruction: Observe that any exchange already in process must finish and is not canceled by this action.
steps.expected_result: Any exchange already in process continues to completion.
steps.healthy_condition: In-process exchanges are allowed to finish rather than being stopped by the bag out action.
steps.failure_condition: An exchange already in process is stopped unexpectedly, or behavior differs from the documented description.

actions.label: Observe in-process exchange completion
actions.exact_action: Observe that any exchange already in process must finish and is not canceled by this action.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The bag out control slide text describing cancel bag out behavior and noting that an item already in exchange must finish.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if an exchange already in process does not continue to completion.
Do not expect an exchange already in process to stop; escalate if observed behavior differs.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
If an item is already in exchange it must finish.

success_criteria: System bag out is initiated from the overall system bag out control.
Totes or chutes containing parcels are exchanged.
Any exchange already in process continues to completion.
healthy_conditions: The overall system bag out control is accessible.
System bag out behavior matches the source description.
All destinations close during the bag out.
Occupied totes or chutes are exchanged.
In-process exchanges finish rather than being canceled.
failure_conditions: The overall system bag out control cannot be accessed.
The system bag out option cannot be selected.
Occupied totes or chutes with parcels are not exchanged.
Observed behavior does not match the documented system bag out description.
An exchange already in process does not continue to completion.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if observed bag out behavior does not match the documented system bag out description.
Escalate if the overall system bag out control is unavailable or inaccessible.
Escalate if destinations do not close or occupied totes/chutes are not exchanged.
Escalate if an exchange already in process does not finish as described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall system bag out control behavior, including system bag out and in-process exchange notes.
Overall system main overview screen showing bag out among system controls.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange chutes or totes with parcels in them.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact button names for the system bag out control.
The source does not provide exact confirmation prompts or UI messages.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing the procedure.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before initiating system bag out.
The source does not specify any LOTO requirement.
The source describes behavior but does not provide a detailed verification screen or status indicator list.
